Hey, Vipul! Completely forgot to mention the advantages of using a vault when it comes to card enrichments.

By enriching cards at the vault level instead of the gateway or acquirer, you avoid running (and paying for) the same Account Updater, 3DS, and Network Token events multiple times across different payment providers.

A single update in the vault automatically propagates to all connected processors, so you only pay for the enrichment once, while every PSP and acquirer benefits from the fresher data.

Worth a chat?

Hi there Vipul!  Wondering if payment solutions are something you are responsible for? 🤔

Would love to share insights with you on how we can assist your platform to optimise payments.

Hey, Vipul! At Basis Theory, we offer an independent payment vault that gives merchants full control over their payment data free from PSP lock-in.

Our platform tokenizes and secures payment data so you can switch processors, enter new markets, or add payment methods without starting from scratch. And because we're PCI Level 1 compliant, we can remove up to 90% of your PCI compliance burden.

Your data stays yours. Also, we see an average uplift of 10-25% in auth rates, and we help our clients to cut processing costs by 30% after they add more PSPs and methods to their payment stack.

Worth a chat?

Hey, Vipul!

Card data lock-in is one of those problems that feels theoretical, until you're staring at a migration bill you can't absorb.

Maxio, a B2B billing platform serving hundreds of subscription businesses, faced exactly that when they wanted to expand into new markets and add payment methods. Their tokens were locked to a single PSP, and moving wasn't just painful; it was a cost they couldn't afford.

With a third-party vault in place, that changed.

Today, they move freely across regions, methods, and currencies, without rebuilding and without touching their customers' experience. And along the way, we helped them save seven figures.

Happy to exchange ideas and see if there's an opportunity to work together, or, at least, gain valuable insights from one another

Hey, Vipul. Great to connect with you!

Took some time on your profile and really liked your background in product and technology. Given your experience, I was wondering if you'd be open to a quick chat about payments and vaulting.

I've been talking to a lot of payment teams lately, single PSP and multi, and the same problem keeps surfacing: when your card data lives in a PSP's vault, you lose the ability to move freely. 

Switching processors becomes a negotiation. Entering new markets gets messy. And if your PSP goes down, your tokens are useless everywhere else.

Would love to swap notes and hear how you're thinking about this on your side. 
What do you say?

CONVERSATION TITLE: Job opportunity: Product Strategy and Operations Manager (hybrid, NYC based)
SUBJECT: Job opportunity: Product Strategy and Operations Manager (hybrid, NYC based)
CONTENT:
Hi Vipul,

This is Flor! Nice to meet you! I came across your profile and wanted to reach out because we’re growing our teams at Canary Technologies (https://www.canarytechnologies.com/), and your experience really stood out.

Canary (https://www.canarytechnologies.com/) is a fast-growing, Y Combinator-backed startup that recently closed an $80M Series D (June 2025). Its AI-powered Guest Management Platform is transforming hospitality, already trusted by 20,000+ hotels in 100+ countries—including brands like Marriott, Wyndham, Best Western, and Four Seasons. 

Canary Technologies is scaling rapidly in the US, and the Product Strategy & Operations team needs a hands-on Product Strategy & Operations Manager to own the analytics infrastructure, operating rhythms, and cross-functional processes that keep the business running and growing. 

You will be the connective tissue between teams, turning data into KPIs, building scalable reporting that leadership relies on. 

If you love building systems that make teams smarter and faster, and you want direct influence on how a high-growth company operates, this is your role. 

Notes: This is a senior role with strong ownership and you will be leading processes but not teams. Also, it is a hands on role with strong SQL experience required.

* JD here [https://jobs.lever.co/canarytechnologies/68d53995-3f2f-4298-a1bb-f5d092911e37]

Total cash compensation:  $140,000 - $180,000. This includes base salary + performance-based bonus that is paid quarterly. This role may also include the opportunity to earn equity. Compensation is subject to standard withholding and applicable taxes. 

The role is hybrid, 3 days in office, 2 days HO.

If it sounds interesting, you can pick a time for a video interview with Paula, from my team, so she can tell you more: https://calendar.app.google/DBdUPL8FqJGJoKuu7 [https://calendar.app.google/DBdUPL8FqJGJoKuu7] (please send me your updated resume before the call).

Looking forward to hearing from you!

Flor Rabinovich
Consultora de Rediseño Profesional | Acompaño a profesionales a repensarse y diseñar sus próximos pasos con coherencia y estrategia | @soyflorrabinovich (IG) / @florrabinovich (Youtube)

Vipul, I’m reaching out selectively regarding a Product Manager role supporting a global consumer brand’s ecommerce team. This is not a generic ecommerce PM search.

The mandate is to own the Conversion & Post-Checkout squad globally (15+ sites) — with a sharp focus on checkout optimization, payment flow efficiency, order validation, and downstream integrations.

Before we schedule time, could you please send:
• An updated resume
 • Ballpark compensation expectations

A few quick alignment questions:

* How many years of direct ownership do you have over checkout and post-checkout experience (not just PDP / upper funnel)?
* How many years have you worked closely with engineering on payment integrations, order validation, or order management system flows?
* How many years have you been actively leveraging AI tools (personally or professionally) to enhance product workflows, experimentation, or digital experience?

If aligned, happy to share more context.

*Visa sponsorship is not available for this role.

Scott Hale
Talent Director | Vlogger - #HaleTalks | KForce Elite Award Recipient 2012 - 2023

Just following up on my previous email to ensure that it found you. Also, I wanted to share a quick success story from a retail brand we support.

King Living, a global furniture retailer founded in Australia, faced rising staffing costs and growing pressure to maintain top-tier customer care. They partnered with us at Satellite Office to build a skilled offshore team in the Philippines.

Here’s what we helped them achieve:
•	Built a dedicated 5-person Customer Care team
•	Improved support performance while reducing operating costs
•	Expanded into finance, eCommerce, sales support, IT, and logistics
•	Scaled to 84 offshore team members in 3 years — and still growing

This kind of strategic offshoring helps retailers stay agile, reduce overhead, and scale key operations effectively. I’ve always believed that if a job can be done remotely, it can also be done more cost-efficiently — without compromising on the quality of talent.

Hi Vipul, 

Thanks for connecting! I advise several early-stage EdTech startups, so what you’re building is right in my niche and interests.

Quick thoughts after looking at FriendlyFeel:
1. Really cool concept: emotional value for parents is high, and it feels like a scalable digital product.
2. My concerns are mostly around B2C challenges... It’s hard to consistently attract paying customers in a crowded space.
3. Retention may be tricky. After a few stories, what keeps families coming back?
4. Defensibility: what prevents another company from replicating this idea quickly?
5. Competition: beyond direct apps, you’re competing with “time” and many other ways parents engage with kids.

Why don’t you send a calendar invite (dbach1725@gmail.com)? Wednesday or Thursday after 5:30 PM EST is ideal, but I can move things around if needed.

To make our conversation more productive, could you quickly share:
1. When did you start FriendlyFeel? Is the company incorporated? Any investors yet?
2. How many employees or co-founders are on the team?
3. What’s the long-term vision for the product?
4. How many active users do you currently have?
5. Anything else I should know before we chat?

Looking forward to connecting!

SUBJECT: Revolutionizing Music Ownership with Web3 + AI – BandRoyalty
CONTENT:
Hi Vipul,

I am building BandRoyalty, a MusicTech and Web 3.0 platform revolutionizing how music is created, owned, published, and monetized. 

Today’s music ecosystem lacks transparency, fair royalties, and meaningful fan-artist interaction. BandRoyalty solves this by combining blockchain, big data, and artificial intelligence to empower artists and fans through decentralized song ownership, Web3-based monetization tools, and AI-enhanced discoverability. Our mission is to create a fairer, more efficient music economy where creators and rights holders have more access and control over their income streams.

Our team has over 100 years of combined music industry experience and currently manages music assets worth several million USD.

We are currently scaling and building an advisory board of leaders who are also interested in participating in our angel funding round.

SUBJECT: AMC Networks - Product Owner (Marketing Technology) - NYC
CONTENT:
Hi Vipul, 

I came across your LinkedIn profile and I thought you would be a great fit for a Product Owner (Marketing Technology) we are hiring for AMC Networks. 

This will be a contract position that is long term, multi-year before converting permanent. This role will be based out of New York City with a flexible hybrid schedule. 

Product Manager who has experience running Marketing Technology focused products within a consumer facing environment. They will be responsible for owning the development, enhancement and delivery of the companies Web and mobile products primarily but will be exposed to OTT, Smart devices and smart TVs. This person will gather requirements while making and maintaining relationships with stake holders. Run sprints, tickets, documentation and troubleshoot needed applications. They should have the ability to research and provide insights and ideas towards new features and ways to enhance existing products. They will work closely with the Marketing team who will direct SEO, CRM and user based enhancements. A focus on customization will be driven from the use of consumer data gathered from Tableau and Mix Panel dashboards.

Must-haves 
- 4+ years of Product Manager experience focused on Marketing Technology, user adoption customization and data analytics  
- Experience working with Martech tools such as AppsFlyer, Branch, Kochava
- Hands on experience working with Tableau or Mix Panel dashboards to analyze user data and deliver insights 
- Experience with A/B testing, customer surveys and gathering customer data
- Experience with requirement gathering and team feature reviews, utilizing Jira and Confluence for documentation and ticketing  
- Experience working on delivery of a web product that has integrated video & audio playback 

Please send over your resume and best phone number to reach you on if you are interested!

Lisa Rey
Lead Recruiter at Insight Global
lisa.rey@insightglobal.com

CONVERSATION TITLE: Transforming Mental Healthcare with Kuddo (Angel Round)
SUBJECT: Transforming Mental Healthcare with Kuddo (Angel Round)
CONTENT:
Hi Vipul,

I am building Kuddo, an AI-powered platform for eating disorder treatment fidelity that helps organizations, clinicians, and parents ensure high-quality mental healthcare at scale.

Mental healthcare is facing a crisis, 80% of youth do not receive adequate care, and even when they do, treatment fidelity and clinician burnout remain major challenges.

Kuddo addresses these challenges by offering real-time fidelity assessment for organizations, AI-driven session structuring to enhance clinician efficiency, and 24/7 AI-powered support with real-time interventions and gamified engagement to improve patient and parent adherence.

Kuddo’s Key Highlights and Developments:
* Raising $1M SAFE round ($250K filled)
* $377K ACV signed in LOIs for pilot programs with 3 private therapy practices
* Founding team from Stanford Medicine (CCO), Harvard Medical School + Mass General Hospital (CTO), Carnegie Mellon (CEO)
* Advisors from Weill Cornell, New York Presbyterian Hospital, Cerebral

My co-founders and I are on a mission to revolutionize mental health care. We are looking for angel investors who share our vision for transforming mental healthcare. If this aligns with your interests, I would love to speak with you.

I would love to discuss how we can work together to bring this vision to life. I have attached our pitch deck [https://hubs.ly/Q033lC-F0] for more details, and you can complete this short form [https://share.hsforms.com/1Y8NUDBjgTD6b0WeygXrfvAnjiff] to express your interest.

2. We're careful about who we sell to, if they meet all the laws/compliances, etc. e.g. some SKU sales are banned to certain countries. For sale to some countries, a specific license is required.

Requiring countries to sign government-to-government agreements outlining measures to prevent smuggling. Maybe you can do POC with OpenAI/anthropic. You need to be willing to stand up the engineering muscle to support customizing your own models on top of Llama etc long term otherwise you cannot differentiate the tech

At max: You have to be able to talk about all subjects 
At min: your company needs to be in control of the “allow list” of what you can talk about 

You get neither if you just use OpenAI/Anthropic.
You don’t control their content policies which is an intrinsic problem + business risk in talk therapy
Main thing I’d say is do not operate under the assumption that you can build on the closed source lab models

Thanks Juanjo, for such a prompt response. Answering your questions below: 
I want to start with the US first and then expand globally. Right now, I am focused on utilizing licensed mental health practitioners to provide 24/7 service to users in need. 
Initially, I want to focus on stress and anxiety and based on the success of the model expand into other modalities. 
I aim to combine AI and human therapists to engage end users and reduce workload for therapists where AI would help them execute their day to day tasks. 
Currently, I am building an MVP to pilot with 3-4 organizations with 100 members each, targeting early 2025 for pilot launch. 
In terms of challenges, I believe gathering supply is the biggest challenge in this domain. 
I envision this platform to become a part of the employee benefits program where employers will be responsible for making payments on behalf of their employees for our services. Moving forward, I do want to customize the platform to meet the needs of universities and schools. 

I come from a technical product management background and I’d say that i need to learn a lot about the subject. I’d be glad to lean on your experience for the same. 
In the mean time, please check out friendlyfeel.com, it’s still a work in progress but it does allow one to reach out to a therapist in realtime or schedule an appointment using HIPPA compliant tools. 
I look forward to learning from you. Please let me know a good time to chat. Thanks

Hi Vipul,

Thank you for reaching out and sharing your vision. A 24/7 mental health platform is an exciting initiative, and it aligns with many of the systemic challenges I’ve been addressing in my work. I’d love to learn more about your goals and how you’re shaping the platform.

To better understand how I might guide or support you, I’d like to ask:

    What’s your target audience? Are you focusing on a specific region, demographic, or type of mental health professional or service user?
    What role do professionals play in your platform? Are you integrating human-led therapy, AI-driven support, or a combination of both?
    What challenges are you currently facing? This could be in terms of technology, adoption, scalability, or governance.
    How do you envision long-term sustainability? Do you see this as a subscription model, a public-private partnership, or something else?

I’d also be happy to share more about my own initiatives, including global collaborations on governance and training, if that resonates. Let me know your availability for a chat—I’m looking forward to exploring this further with you.

Warm regards,

Dear Vipul, 

I am a Neonatologist and CEO/Founder of Firstday Healthcare [https://www.firstday.health/], an innovative hospital at home platform, inspired by the success of the adult hospital at home movement but created for the specific unmet needs of the pediatric population with medical complexity. 

Our goal is clear: to support personalized, predictive, and value based healthcare delivery for children addressing their unique medical needs in the comfort of their home from the first days of life, throughout childhood, and beyond. The platform integrates cutting edge technologies including wireless remote patient monitoring using third party FDA approved vital sensors, telemedicine, pediatric specific care workflows, and a digital healthcare record companion to ensure that every child receives the highest quality care.

We are currently recruiting advisory board members who are also interested in participating in our angel funding round.

To provide you with a comprehensive understanding of our work, I encourage you to explore our deck [https://docsend.com/view/u2ctrzz5k44wudpq].

CONVERSATION TITLE: Mental Health Telemedicine Startup Focused on Medicaid - Advisory Board + Angel Round
SUBJECT: Mental Health Telemedicine Startup Focused on Medicaid - Advisory Board + Angel Round
CONTENT:
Hello Vipul,

I am the CEO of Esteem Health, a healthcare startup focused on providing mental health services via telemedicine to Medicaid’s $174B market of underserved communities. We serve preschoolers, tweens, high school students, college students, young adults, and families.

We’re recruiting advisory board members who are passionate about our mission of improving the mental health of underserved communities through telemedicine. 

We are ideally looking for advisory board members that would be interested in lending 1-2 hours per month of their time to the company and that would also consider participating as an angel investor in our company. 

Please consider joining our advisory board and participating as an angel to help us scale our company and help thousands of Medicaid patients. 

Responsibilities of advisors include: 
Providing feedback and guidance to the management team & other advisors (via email, Zoom, and Basecamp) on company growth and strategies.
Making helpful introductions to potential customers or partners.

Benefits to advisors include: 
Being issued stock options in exchange for spending a few hours on the company each month
Participate as an angel via an IRA/401K 


Why join us now?
There is a significant need within the Medicaid population for mental health services that are accessible through telemedicine with more than 63% (22M) Medicaid members diagnosed with a mental health disorder having gone without any care. 
 
Esteem Health has demonstrated viability with contracts from managed care organizations and has a proven ability to receive Medicaid reimbursement. 

If you are interested in joining our mission improving the mental health of Medicaid populations through the power of telemedicine, please apply to join our advisory board here: https://bit.ly/3Rj2xX8

Please review our pitch deck here: https://bit.ly/49UkVN6

Best,

Austin Miller
CEO, Esteem Health

CONVERSATION TITLE: Tech Lead, Ecommerce Opportunity (Remote)
SUBJECT: Tech Lead, Ecommerce Opportunity (Remote)
CONTENT:
Our client, a cutting edge digital agency, is looking to bring on a contract Tech Lead, eCommerce to support their growing client portfolio! Role will 100% remote, require around 30-40 hours per week, and has potential to convert to fulltime.

Type: Contract
Location: Fully Remote
Start: January 22nd
Hours: 30-40/week
Pay Range: $70-75/hr.

Overview:
The successful candidate will have a proven track record of leading eCommerce implementation projects using platforms such as Sitecore, Optimizely, or ZNode. With a strong background in technology and 6+ years of experience, the Technology Lead will drive the success of our eCommerce projects by providing technical expertise and guidance throughout the entire development process.

Responsibilities:
Lead and manage eCommerce implementation projects using Sitecore, Optimizely, or ZNode platforms.
Collaborate with project managers, business analysts, and developers to define project scope, goals, and deliverables.
Provide technical guidance and expertise to team members throughout the project life cycle, ensuring adherence to best practices and industry standards.
Analyze client requirements and design solutions that meet both their needs and strategic objectives.
Develop project plans, timelines, and budgets, and monitor progress to ensure timely delivery and successful outcomes.
Identify and mitigate potential risks and roadblocks to project success.
Communicate effectively with clients and team members, keeping them informed of project progress, challenges, and achievements.
Conduct code reviews, ensuring high-quality deliverables and adherence to coding standards.
Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues in a timely and efficient manner.
Maintain up-to-date knowledge of eCommerce trends, technologies, and best practices.

If interested please send updated resume + availability for next week.

Keighly Clapp
Account Manager at 24 Seven Talent

Roger that, Vipul.  You’d want to reach out to someone involved in Strategic Operations - which I am not.  

I ingest all new products and processes and work with Product and ENG to create internal and external product blueprints to address any & all potential unhappy paths - front of site and in-house resources for customers and internal employees.

Community Operations and Steategic Operations are completely separate.  Strat Ops deals with the client acquisition side of things, i.e., leads/onboarding/new products that the current healthcare space is demanding/prioritizing client needs against ENG bandwidth, pluses and deltas, etc.

I’m completely detached from Strat Ops in my day-to-day, but they certainly get a lot of our future initiatives and align on prioritization through many unique avenues: current client feedback/past client feedback/staying tuned in to healthcare provider networks to better understand their pain points… what they like about us vs the competitors/what we can offer that would set us apart from the same competitors, etc.

Thanks Christopher. I understand you are not involved with the hiring side of things.

Hi Vipul

I hope you are safe and doing well! Here is how recruiting works at Walmart- You either have to apply on Walmart's portal (you can google it) or through LinkedIn/Glassdoor jobs. 

Once you apply, it takes you to a Workday application page. If there is an option of referral source, you need to provide your connection's Walmart email ID. If you don't have any connection in Walmart, let me know - I can provide you a referral (my email ID: Amrit.Mohapatra@walmart.com). Unfortunately, there is no other way of providing referral or forward any candidate's profile to recruiters.

Currently, my team doesn't have any openings, so I won't be able to send your resume to my Hiring Manager / Recruiter.

My Suggestion - Check the portal/LinkedIn company page for multiple data analytics roles and straightaway apply for those that deem suitable to you. IMHO - referrals in Walmart don't carry much weightage (unless a team member/someone at a director or above level provides referral for you).

Good luck with your job search and do let me know if I can help you with anything else!

Stay safe!
Amrit

I hope you’re doing well.

We are seeking Technical Product/AI Product Delivery Director/Manager to lead project delivery teams & is responsible for both frontline impact and client satisfaction. You will report directly to the Sr. VP- AI Product Delivery (Global).

11. Overall efforts to ensure higher operating margins, greater stakeholder returns, increased customer engagement and achieving business goals.

For me the most important is the quality education that i am looking forward to seeing the curriculum , a great brand on my resume and opportunities in that order.

You will have to make that decision for yourself, there will be struggles but you have to decide what you want out of the course, is it the brand, opportunities in the US or a worthy job after any course that you pursue

Re: dates to apply, think about timing as one of many leverage variables. If you think you are in a strong position for this role, then timing doesn't matter, you should do it right away and then negotiate or stall the conversation for timing.

Now, if instead you think you could use all the help you can to get this job, you can wait until timing is better. But if you are in that position, I don't think that's going to change much if it's a month or two difference.

I personally would never wait to apply for anything. If you are the right fit, timing can be negotiated very easily. If not, it won't help.

(My comments are in general, not about Asana, but it also applies there)

3- A career experiences survey where you can indicate your work experiences and interest in specific activities or areas of responsibility in various MBA roles at Amazon.
4- An experiences and preferences survey which asks about your previous experiences, skills, and preferred approach to work related to a variety of general and specific technical skills.
5- A role preferences survey where you are asked to provide your preferences for the MBA roles currently available.

These had helped when I was preparing. Not necessarily applicable to Meta but should help.

1. For Product Metrics, I studied “Product Manager Interview” by Lewis C Lin. Not the entire book, just the chapters relevant to the company I was applying for. You might find an online pdf somewhere.

2. Look at Product School videos on LinkedIn/YouTube. You'll have company specific PMs providing talks.

3. For Product Analytics Frameworks, I followed this: https://youtu.be/nPJKFWMiIC8 Didn't do anything beyond this as it's quite exhaustive and good.

4. For A/B Testing, I used the framework from here: https://towardsdatascience.com/cracking-a-b-testing-data-science-interviews-bc66e399b109 It's really good and by far the best resource I've seen.

5. SQL I didn’t practice at all but Window Functions and self-joins are important concepts. mode.com is a good resource to revise concepts. This post is also solid: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/eric-weber-060397b7_data-datascience-sql-activity-6837346925180706816-QXl2

6. Apart from these, revise your basic statistics and probability. Any Udemy/Udacity/Coursera/Khan Academy course should be good for this. I really like this one: https://www.coursera.org/learn/inferential-statistics-intro?specialization=statistics
Don't think you'll need to go very deep into Probability. Bayes Theorem and its applications should be good enough.

^These resources should be good enough for you to generally crack tech companies. Of course, doing company specific prep from Glassdoor and Interview Query will also help.

These had helped when I was preparing. Not necessarily applicable to Meta but should help.

1. For Product Metrics, I studied “Product Manager Interview” by Lewis C Lin. Not the entire book, just the chapters relevant to the company I was applying for. You might find an online pdf somewhere.

2. Look at Product School videos on LinkedIn/YouTube. You'll have company specific PMs providing talks.

3. For Product Analytics Frameworks, I followed this: https://youtu.be/nPJKFWMiIC8 Didn't do anything beyond this as it's quite exhaustive and good.

4. For A/B Testing, I used the framework from here: https://towardsdatascience.com/cracking-a-b-testing-data-science-interviews-bc66e399b109 It's really good and by far the best resource I've seen.

5. SQL I didn’t practice at all but Window Functions and self-joins are important concepts. mode.com is a good resource to revise concepts. This post is also solid: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/eric-weber-060397b7_data-datascience-sql-activity-6837346925180706816-QXl2

6. Apart from these, revise your basic statistics and probability. Any Udemy/Udacity/Coursera/Khan Academy course should be good for this. I really like this one: https://www.coursera.org/learn/inferential-statistics-intro?specialization=statistics
Don't think you'll need to go very deep into Probability. Bayes Theorem and its applications should be good enough.

^These resources should be good enough for you to generally crack tech companies. Of course, doing company specific prep from Glassdoor and Interview Query will also help.

These had helped when I was preparing. Not necessarily applicable to Meta but should help.

1. For Product Metrics, I studied “Product Manager Interview” by Lewis C Lin. Not the entire book, just the chapters relevant to the company I was applying for. You might find an online pdf somewhere.

2. Look at Product School videos on LinkedIn/YouTube. You'll have company specific PMs providing talks.

3. For Product Analytics Frameworks, I followed this: https://youtu.be/nPJKFWMiIC8 Didn't do anything beyond this as it's quite exhaustive and good.

4. For A/B Testing, I used the framework from here: https://towardsdatascience.com/cracking-a-b-testing-data-science-interviews-bc66e399b109 It's really good and by far the best resource I've seen.

5. SQL I didn’t practice at all but Window Functions and self-joins are important concepts. mode.com is a good resource to revise concepts. This post is also solid: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/eric-weber-060397b7_data-datascience-sql-activity-6837346925180706816-QXl2

6. Apart from these, revise your basic statistics and probability. Any Udemy/Udacity/Coursera/Khan Academy course should be good for this. I really like this one: https://www.coursera.org/learn/inferential-statistics-intro?specialization=statistics
Don't think you'll need to go very deep into Probability. Bayes Theorem and its applications should be good enough.

^These resources should be good enough for you to generally crack tech companies. Of course, doing company specific prep from Glassdoor and Interview Query will also help.

Hey Vipul, nice to meet you! We're hiring across a few roles:

- Working on growth and platforms for our network quality data tools for service providers - benefits from a B2B/SaaS background.

- Privacy product management for the same data tools - also SaaS, but familiarity with data privacy is critical.

- Subscriber acquisition products - benefits from work on ads products or marketing efforts + products at CSPs.

- Consumer facing products to help people understand and optimize their connections - requires background in consumer mobile apps.

- Network infrastructure optimization software - applicable to anyone who has done software product management for infrastructure applications.

CONVERSATION TITLE: Remote Growth Marketing Manager - Paid Search
SUBJECT: Remote Growth Marketing Manager - Paid Search
CONTENT:
Are you interested in a new remote Growth Marketing Manager position? If so, please email your resume to roeshaun.murry@shipt.com to learn more about the position.

Shipt is looking for a Paid Search Growth Marketing Manager to join our member acquisition team. You will play an imperative role in shaping and implementing our paid media strategy and performance marketing tactics to drive new customer experiences. 

Responsibilities

Manage and coordinate all Paid Search implementation across Google, Bing and other search engines to increase membership acquisition

Proactively review and identify Paid Search growth opportunities by analyzing existing content architecture, keyword constructs, audits and data/traffic analysis

Partner with other marketing managers and agencies to support and meet the growth marketing team’s goals

Provide weekly data performance recaps including actionable insights and recommendations

Define measurable goals and critical metrics, set priorities, socialize Paid Search strategy, and work with partner teams to meet those requirements

Monitor, analyze and optimize performance towards key business metrics

Partner with creative and brand marketing team members to develop strong messaging and storytelling for direct response ads; provide a constant feedback loop on creative performance to inform new ideas

Plan interactive testing and run experiments on keywords, copy, and landing pages

Perform market research, investigate benchmarks, and identify new opportunities for growth

Stay informed about Paid Search trends, search engine algorithm updates, and technology

Job Requirements

4+ years of strong experience managing online acquisition channels, digital marketing experience working for a marketplace company or subscription model a plus (eg: DoorDash, Instacart, Uber, Lyft, GrubHub, etc).

Shun James-Murry
Professional Recruiter at Shipt

Square requires minimum 3 years of PM experience for all roles, and most roles require minimum 5 years of PM experience. Most PM roles in similar size companies require similar prior experience. Besides APM programs the other option would be to join startups and work with them to structure your responsibilities, if you want the PM title.

Hi Vipul, if I can be transparent with you, the best option is to recruit for roles where you have prior experience. It'll be a stretch to get product management roles, and it's very rare to see roles that don't require prior PM experience. If you want to get into product management the best option would be junior PMs / APMs at companies that have such programs like Google, Intuit, Facebook and Amazon. But they come with significantly lower salaries than analyst roles that you'd get based on your experience.

CONVERSATION TITLE: Venture Leader Opportunity
SUBJECT: Venture Leader Opportunity
CONTENT:
Hi Vipul, I’m a talent sourcer with HireNetworks. We’ve been contracted to fill a Venture Leader position for one of our clients and I think with your background you could be a fit.

Our client is seeking an entrepreneurial minded leader for a new business unit. Reporting to the President, you would have ownership of all areas of the business unit and ensure the development of commercial and technical solutions. Your responsibilities would encompass technical solution and business model development, market traction and sales development, venture financial forecast and budgeting and leadership of the venture team. 

This is a direct hire, full time position on site in Raleigh, NC. The compensation range for this position is $90-100k.

If you’re interested, let me know. I’d like to connect you with the senior recruiter assigned to this role to discuss it and your background. 

Thanks!

Andy Osborne, Talent Sourcing Specialist
O: 919.981.6200 x324
aosborne@hirenetworks.com

SUBJECT: Hiring Business Analyst (Master’s degree Business management with minimum 3+ years of experience – Top Tier schools only (Top 10 in India/abroad) for Gurgaon office
CONTENT:
We are looking for Analytics Consultants who are energetic and curious, who will help us discover and apply insights from vast amounts of data and advise all stakeholders in making smarter decisions. 
 
Responsibilities - What you will be expected to bring to the team:
1. Experience delivering data & analytics advisory engagements, likely coupled with specific industry sector knowledge 
- Specific sector knowledge across Supply Chain, Industry 4.0, Consumer Retail, Finance, Healthcare, TMT etc. would be given additional weightage
2. Practical experience of using your knowledge and analytical skills to help organizations achieve tangible results
3. Possess technical expertise and sector knowledge combined with project delivery (+ project management) experience, enabling you to understand business needs and to implement tailored solutions.
Domain Skills - You will have knowledge and/or experience in a selection of the following domain areas:
1. Data Science: Advanced Learning Algorithms –machine learning; deep learning; Automation – robotics; process improvement; Programming languages- R, Python, SQL
2. Digital Customer Analytics: Customer insight, segmentation, marketing & channel optimization, social analytics, customer lifecycle analytics, customer journeys, web analytics, campaign targeting & measurement
3. Platforms: Architectural / feature knowledge – Google Cloud Platform; Amazon Web Services; Microsoft Azure; Salesforce Einstein; Adobe Sensei
4. Operational Analytics:  Driving operational improvement and minimizing risk – supply chain analytics, people and process insights, asset investment planning, operational risk prediction
5. Data Visualization: advanced visualization tools – Power BI, Tableau, Qlik; storytelling using data; dashboard design; MI/BI best practice; creating communications content
6. AI and Data strategy: data governance; data culture change;

Vidhami Mishra

SUBJECT: RE: Opportunity with Airtel Payments Bank
CONTENT:
Hi Vipul, 

Thanks for the application, please find below the job description: 

Purpose of the Job
An individual performing the role of a Business Analyst focuses on converting requirements into the architecture and design that ultimately constitute the blueprint for the solution. In that process relies on design patterns from their previous engagements, published reference architectures, and on guidance from enterprise architecture. In their efforts, solution architects balance architectural concerns of the projects with the concerns of the enterprise
 
Deliverables (Maximum 5-6 key responsibilities)
Requirement Analysis
1)           Understand the business requirements for an initiative.
2)           Evolve the business requirements by a discussion with business teams and doing a feasibility analysis.
Solution Development
3)           Work with the development team to create a detailed technical solution and document the same.
4)           Get the solution document signed off.
Delivery and User Acceptance Test
5)           Work with the development team for any clarifications on business requirements during the development phase.
6)           Be part of the UAT and help clarify the bugs from new requirements.
Innovation
7)           Be updated with the latest in the technology happening in the payments space.
 
Major Challenges :
1)           Keep abreast with latest in the technology and provide the most relevant solutions.
2)           Work with multiple teams to evolve a solution.
 
Demonstrate (Key competencies)
>Consulting, Facilitation and Selling skills
>Basic knowledge of development
>Teamwork and collaboration
>Networking and interpersonal skills
>Leadership qualities
>Enterprising and entrepreneurial
>Results orientation

CONVERSATION TITLE: Exclusive to Duke Alumni and Friends l Towerview Ventures
CONTENT:
Hi Vipul,

 

I am the Managing Partner of Towerview Ventures, a venture capital fund for Duke alumni and friends. With over 18,000 Duke alumni having joined our community, I’m continuing to reach out and spread the word.

 

Towerview Ventures is powered by Alumni Ventures, one of the most active venture capital firms in the world (Pitchbook, 2021). We make venture capital simple and accessible for millions of accredited investors who have previously been shut out of this important asset class. Here’s how:

 

  • Investing in our fund starts at $25K. Many VC firms require $5M+ to even get started.
  • With a single investment, we provide you with a fund of ~25-30 vetted venture-backed companies, diversified by stage, sector, and geography.
  • We only invest alongside well-known VC firms, like Sequoia, Kleiner Perkins, NEA, and others.* 

Together we are demystifying and democratizing venture capital. Join the community to receive our newsletter, learn about VC and amazing Duke founders, and, if you choose, invest with us. Building on the success of our first three funds, we are now raising Fund 4. Performance data is available upon request.

 

If you are interested in learning more about investing or simply wish to follow our progress, click to sign up.

 

Greg Baker (Fuqua ‘91)

Managing Partner, Towerview Ventures

CONVERSATION TITLE: Duke Alumni: Add VC to your investment portfolio
CONTENT:
Hi Vipul,

 

The savviest institutional investors have a balanced, diversified portfolio consisting of a number of asset classes, including venture capital. Do you? Venture capital can improve the overall risk-return profile of your portfolio through lower volatility, further diversification, and reduced correlation to traditional asset classes.1 Until recently, it was extraordinarily difficult for individuals to access compelling venture funds for their portfolio. 

 

As the Managing Partner of Towerview Ventures, I’d like to personally welcome you to our community of over 13,000 Duke alumni & friends learning about and investing in venture capital. Towerview Ventures is powered by Alumni Ventures, America’s largest venture capital firm for individual investors.2 We make venture capital simple and accessible. Investing in our fund of ~23-27 vetted venture-backed companies starts at $25K and can be funded from many existing retirement accounts.

 

This year’s fund is quickly coming to a close. Click below to learn about venture capital investing, amazing Duke founders, and, if you choose, invest with us.

 

Greg Baker (Fuqua ‘91)

Managing Partner, Towerview Ventures

 

 

1 BCG, The Future is Private, March 2022

2 Alumni Ventures is America’s largest VC firm for individual investors based on the combination of total capital raised, number of investments and number of investors of leading VC firms as reported by Pitchbook and other publicly available information reviewed by AV.

CONVERSATION TITLE: Everything You Can Expect From the Application Process
FROM: Purdue University Krannert School of Management
CONTENT:
Hi Vipul,


You've already decided on the right Master's program for your professional goals—Now it's time to begin the application process. The first step is to create an online profile with Purdue University's Graduate School. Next, you'll need to start gathering the documents necessary to complete your submission before applications close.


You'll need to prepare:

- Essay(s): Each program has specific requirements for the number of essays and topics you'll be writing during the application process. 

- Two Online Letters of Recommendation

- Official Transcripts

- Video Essays: You will need to answer 3-5 prompts via video response. Topics will vary based on the program you are applying to.

- GMAT/GRE Scores: GMAT/GRE waivers may also be awarded to domestic applicants with 4+ years of professional experience and/or sufficient educational experience as decided by the application committee. A separate application is required to receive a waiver.


Application Deadlines for Krannert's Summer Start Master's Programs:

- Early Application: September 1 - November 1

- Priority Round: November 2 - January 3

- Final International Round: January 4 - March 1 (January 31 for MSBAIM)

- Final Domestic Round: February 1 - April 1*


*Krannert's MS BAIM program accepts applications until April 1 or classes are full


Are you ready to begin the journey toward your Master's degree? Submit your application today to be one step closer to the career you've always dreamed of!


Best,


The Purdue Krannert Team

CONVERSATION TITLE: a 22,500% ROI opportunity, despite the recession?
CONTENT:
Hi Vipul,


You listed Financial Investing as one of your interests, so you surely know that in Internet-related IPOs one can make millions of dollars on a small investment. For example, Mike Walsh and Oren Michels earned $25M million each on their $5K investments in Uber. Such investment opportunities are rarely accessible to individual investors and investment banks and VC funds keep the best deals to themselves.


IT businesses can grow remarkably fast. Zoom has reached the valuation of $140B, TikTok - $75B, Salesforce - $225B.


At TransparentBusiness, we decided to open our Global Private Offering to individuals in order to create a large international network of "brand ambassadors" and prepare the company for a successful IPO next year. Here's the nature of the opportunity I offer for your consideration:


Four billion people have been ordered to stay home due to the coronavirus pandemic and executives around the world are discovering that managing remote workers blindly is as tricky as conducting an orchestra without seeing or hearing the musicians. Never before a market as large has appeared so fast.


We have a solution. It’s robust, acclaimed, and infinitely scalable. Designated by Citigroup as "the Top People Management Solution", our TransparentBusiness platform boosts the productivity of remote work by making it easy to monitor and coordinate. Our investors include current and former executives of Morgan Stanley, Merrill Lynch, J.P. Morgan, Stifel, Bank of America, Barclays Global Investors, UBS, Wells Fargo, Goldman Sachs, Citigroup, Deutsche Bank, Microsoft, Telefonica, EY, Deloitte, IBM, LinkedIn, Microsoft and Accenture. Our objective is to make TransparentBusiness synonymous with the categories of Business Transparency and remote workforce management, globally.


The minimum investment is $5,000 and the maximum is $1M per person. The current stock price is $1 per share. As outlined in our fundraising strategy summary, we plan to do a mini-IPO at the end of this year at $2 per share and the full-IPO on the New York Stock Exchange in the Fourth Quarter of the next year at $10 per share. At TransparentBusiness.com/invest you can review the detailed information about this opportunity and purchase our equity while this round remains open.


Looking forward to hearing from you,


With very best wishes,


Alex Konanykhin

CONVERSATION TITLE: Are you prepared for the post-covid economy in Data Science?
CONTENT:
Hello Vipul,


Every day I see new headlines about the effect that COVID-19 has had on the industry. It’s put us all in a state of uncertainty about the job market and industry prospects. But this isn’t the first time I’ve faced this kind of situation. 


In 2008, it was a similar situation when the recession happened. Nobody knew what the future was going to be like, but if you look back, you’ll know that the industry bounced back. That’s because we became more data-driven, and were able to deliver better business outcomes as a result. That’s why data scientists are in such high demand right now, as companies try to optimize and do more with less. 


To help professionals learn the right Data Science skills to future-proof their careers, we’d launched the PG Program in Data Science and Business Analytics, in collaboration with The University of Texas at Austin. 


It’s been ranked as India’s #1 Program for 5 consecutive years due to exceptional faculty, dedicated learning support, industry-relevant curriculum and career assistance. The program is designed for working professionals to gain the skills they’ll need to build rewarding careers. 


If you’ve been looking to start your Data Science career and have not been sure where to start, this program might be what you’re looking for. You can see for yourself and learn more from the brochure.


Happy Learning!
